mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a linear regression against FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand that reduces corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a mean forward return below zero and p-value exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% forces markets to anticipate restrictive monetary policy, lowering equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% MoM are followed by positive 20-day returns in over 60% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and declining retail sales signals weakening consumer spending power that immediately pressures equity prices, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS changes fails to predict negative 5-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts during a post-shock recovery phase signal renewed construction activity and wealth effects that lift equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases during post-shock recovery periods fail to predict positive 20-day returns, with a mean forward return below zero and p-value exceeding 0.10.
